changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
Changed k8s_audit_log_retention_configured to k8s_audit_log_retention_policy_set for specificity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-mapped to the NIST 800-53 AU-10(1) control, focusing on non-repudiation through audit logging and identity association via authentication and authorization. However, minor naming adjustments were made for clarity and specificity. The function names now better reflect the configuration aspect of audit logging and retention policies.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate configuration status, such as using 'configured' or 'policy_set' to denote active settings.
title: Non-Repudiation - Non - repudiation | association of identities (a) bind the identity of the infor
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 AU-10(1) control focuses on non-repudiation and the association of identities with actions. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to audit logging and the configuration of authentication and authorization mechanisms. Audit logging ensures that actions are recorded and can be traced back to an identity, while proper authentication and authorization settings ensure that identities are correctly associated with their actions.
